entry.title: Re: SAP B1 Alerts
entry.content:
Hi @mouaz_altabbaa974011, this is a common encoding issue where the mail relay doesn't recognize the UTF-8 Arabic characters and reverts to ??????.

First, check your Job Service Configurator and ensure the SMTP encoding is explicitly set to UTF-8. If you are using an older version of the SBO Mailer, consider updating it, as legacy versions often struggle with Unicode in the email body. For a more robust "Clean Core" setup, you might consider routing these alerts through the SAP Alert Notification service on BTP, which provides better native support for multi-language character sets and allows you to validate the payload through Tosca API Testing before it reaches the customer.

entry.title: What is the standard Supplier Confirmation process in SAP IBP (PA – SAPIBP2) ? Any std KF available?
entry.content:
Use Case for Supplier Forecast and Confirmation with SAP IBP (Planning Area – HPA)

Case: The company publishes finished goods forecasts to suppliers.  Suppliers are modeled as locations in IBP to enable capacity planning at supplier product level.

An unconstrained Order-Based Planning (OBP) run is executed to generate total demand-driven capacity requirements at the supplier location, providing visibility of required vs available capacity. Based on this, the supplier reviews the load and updates feasible supply commitments at supplier product level.

Subsequently, a constrained OBP run is executed incorporating supplier capacity limits and commitments, resulting in a realistic, capacity-feasible supply plan aligned with supplier capabilities.

This process enables improved collaboration, capacity visibility, and achievable supply commitments between OEM and suppliers.

Challenge 1:  

  • There is no standard key figure (Marked Red box) available in SAP IBP to represent the supplier-facing forecast that can be shared with suppliers in alignment with best practices.
  • Similarly, there is no standard key figure available to capture supplier confirmations/commitments as an input for the constrained planning run in line with best practices.

Existing  Process Flow :

 

 

 

 

 

Challenge 2

  • As shown in the screenshot, capacity consumption from planned and production orders is available only at the resource level, where multiple products share the same resource. However, in the current HPA Order-Based Planning setup, it is not possible to constrain the production plan specifically at the resource, location, and product combination level.

entry.title: Re: SmartRecruiters / SuccessFactors – Integration scopin...
entry.content:
Hi Barbara, these are excellent scoping questions. To address your core technical points:

  • Independence of Integrations: Yes, you can absolutely deploy Foundational Data Sync (FDS) independently of the Create Job from Position integration. If your client prefers creating requisitions directly in SmartRecruiters, FDS ensures that the picklists and organizational values (Company, Department, Location) remain synchronized with SuccessFactors as the system of record.

  • Prefilling Fields: When creating jobs directly in SmartRecruiters without Position Management, fields are typically populated by selecting from the synced reference values. To "automatically" prefill without a position link, you would usually rely on SmartRecruiters Job Templates or custom mapping logic in your middleware (like SAP BTP or Boomi) to default values based on the recruiter's business unit or the chosen Job Profile.

  • Workstream Separation: You are correct—Employee Central (Core HR) and Onboarding (ONB) are distinct workstreams with different API endpoints, permission sets, and data objects (Job Information vs. Onboarding Candidate Info).

  • Indicative Effort (Rough Ranges):

    • Foundational Data Sync: 3–5 days (basic setup and picklist alignment).

    • Create Job from Position: 5–10 days (requires Position Management alignment).

    • Advanced Field Mapping: 5–15 days (depends heavily on the number of custom fields and transformation logic).

    • Onboarding Integration: 10–20 days (complex due to data privacy, document triggers, and cross-system validation).

For long-term governance, I recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to track these separate workstreams and validating the "integration seams" via Tricentis Tosca to ensure that SuccessFactors releases don't disrupt your custom field mappings.

entry.title: SAP standard function modules which are in not released status

Could you please advise on:

  • Recommended released APIs (e.g., BAPIs, fubctions, released classes/methods, or other whitelisted interfaces) that can replace the unreleased function modules
  • Any standard tools or documentation to help map unreleased function modules to released equivalents

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAP standard function modules which are in not releas...
entry.content:
Hello,

thank you for your request regarding non-released function modules.

For your use case, I recommend using the BAPI_CTRACCONTRACTACCOUNT_CH1 instead of the non-released function module VKK_FICA_DI. General information about non-released function modules can be found in SAP Note 109533.

To identify related released BAPIs, you have two options:

  • Use the where-used list in transaction SE37 by entering the function module to be replaced (e.g., VKK_FICA_DI) and search for "BAPI" in the results.
  • Use transaction SE37 with F4-help to search for released BAPI function modules by name pattern (e.g., BAPI_CTRAC* in contract accounting).

Alternatively, you can contact your implementation partner for further guidance on released objects available for customer use.

Best regards,
Ronny
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: MRP Rescheduling Creates New Purchase Order Instead o...
entry.content:
Hi @alice_henrique70a956, this often happens if the requirement date is outside your Rescheduling Horizon. MRP sees a shortage on 02/06, but because the PO is on 01/07, it may be outside the 'window' where the system is allowed to pull receipts forward.

Check your horizon settings in SSCUI 102144 (for Public Cloud) or OMDT (for Private). Also, to maintain a Clean Core, I'd recommend validating this logic using Tricentis Tosca to ensure that your lot-sizing and horizons remain consistent after S/4HANA upgrades.

entry.title: Re: S_ALR_87013336 dumps with SQL parser error after upgr...
entry.content:
Hi @Balamut, this SQL parser error often occurs because the generated ABAP code from your 1809 system is no longer compatible with the unified table structures in S/4HANA 2025.

To fix this, go to transaction GR55, enter your report group, and select Generate from the menu. This will refresh the dynamic SQL to match the current database schema. I also recommend checking SAP Note 2999120, which specifically addresses SQL parser issues in Report Writer after major upgrades.

Moving forward, it’s a "Clean Core" best practice to use SAP Cloud ALM to track these report regenerations as part of your upgrade cutover plan and to validate them using Tricentis Tosca to ensure they stay functional through future feature pack updates.

entry.title: Re: CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO returns empty data after upgr...
entry.content:
Hi @Balamut, your suspicion is spot on. In S/4HANA 2025, FAGL_LINE_ITEM_BROWSER leverages ALV IDA, which processes data on the database layer and bypasses the hooks used by CL_SALV_BS_RUNTIME_INFO.

To stay aligned with a Clean Core strategy, I recommend moving away from the SUBMIT approach. Instead, consume the underlying CDS Views directly in your ABAP code or use the Fiori-native APIs provided for G/L line items.

If you must use the report logic, check if there is an available BAPI or a Public Class that provides the data. You can also use SAP Cloud ALM to document this technical debt and validate the new data retrieval logic with Tricentis Tosca to ensure it remains stable across future S/4HANA releases.

entry.title: Re: Reschedule exam (new format) to an earlier date
entry.content:
Hi @mariagiovanna, you can definitely move your exam to an earlier date! Just go to your "My Learning" dashboard on the SAP Learning Hub and select "Reschedule".

Just a reminder: make sure to finalize the change at least 24 hours before your original slot to avoid losing the attempt. If you're looking for an earlier date that isn't currently showing, try checking the portal a few times a day—slots often open up when others reschedule. Good luck with your C_ADBTP certification!

entry.title: Re: EHP8 vs EHP7
entry.content: Hi Sanjeev Sinha, maybe this page can help you: HR Support Package | SAP Help Portal It lists the features provided in the different SPs of  EHP8.  There are some payroll related features, mainly related to payroll control center. BR Peter

entry.title: Re: Updating Stock Type / System Status in Material Seria...
entry.content:
Hi @edoymaner, you've hit a standard system constraint. Fields like Stock Type and System Status are strictly transaction-driven to maintain a Clean Core.

You cannot PATCH them directly because they must stay in sync with the physical inventory. Instead of the Serial Number API, use the API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT to post a transfer or movement. When you include the serial number in the material document payload, SAP will update those fields for you automatically. For long-term governance, I'd recommend tracking these status transitions in SAP Cloud ALM and validating the flow with Tosca API Testing to ensure no "orphan" serial numbers are created during high-volume movements.

entry.title: Re: Poland KSeF eDocument Cockpit - "The data object coul...
entry.content:
Hi @JoseGuill, I completely understand your frustration—this is a high-stakes "Clean Core" issue where the platform's internal mapping appears to be broken in your Production tenant.

The double space in the error usually means the variable for the Type Name is initial (null) at runtime. This often happens if the AIF Metadata or the eDocument Proxy wasn't fully activated during the production cutover.

Have you verified if there is a difference in the Feature Toggle status between the two systems? Sometimes a specific KSeF "Early Adopter" toggle is active in Test but was missed in Production. I’d also recommend using SAP Cloud ALM to document this discrepancy and escalate it as a "System Inconsistency" rather than a configuration error to get it routed to the development team faster.

entry.title: Re: Bug regression regarding WSL in SAP CDS Language Supp...
entry.content:
Hi @JohannesZinnau, this definitely looks like a regression where the extension is losing its "WSL-awareness" during the 9.9 update. The error confirms that the cds-typer is being invoked in a Windows context while looking for a Linux environment string.

Aside from downgrading, try running cds build --for typescript manually in your WSL terminal to keep your types updated. I’d also recommend reporting this on the CAP GitHub or through a support ticket, as this breaks the "Clean Core" local development experience. You can use SAP Cloud ALM to track these extension bugs within your team's development environment to ensure everyone stays on the stable 9.8 version until a fix is released.

entry.title: Re: Is this a sign SAP is losing the AI race?
entry.content:
This is a powerful analysis, @joao_sousa2. You've identified the core challenge: the difference between technical governance and market protectionism.

If Joule is to succeed, it must become an "Expert Guide" that adds value general LLMs can't touch, such as native understanding of complex SAP financial and logistics structures. Mandating Joule via A2A protocols might solve the stability issue, but it risks alienating developers who are already seeing massive productivity gains from "open" AI tools like Claude.

Moving forward, the most successful implementations will likely be those that use SAP Cloud ALM to govern these new "integration seams" while pushing SAP to make Joule more capable through deep-tier skills and better support for local/hybrid AI models.
